= rpk cluster config set
:description: Set a single cluster configuration property. This command is provided for use in scripts.
:page-platforms: linux,darwin

// This content is autogenerated. Do not edit manually. To customize content, see the writer's guide: https://github.com/redpanda-data/docs-extensions-and-macros/blob/main/docs-data/RPK_OVERRIDES_GUIDE.adoc

// tag::single-source[]
Set a single cluster configuration property.

This command is provided for use in scripts.  For interactive editing, or bulk
changes, use the `edit` and `import` commands respectively.

You may also use <key>=<value> notation for setting configuration properties:

[,bash]
----
rpk cluster config set log_retention_ms=-1
----

If an empty string is given as the value, the property is reset to its default.
Use the flag `--no-confirm` to avoid the confirmation prompt.

ifdef::env-cloud[]
The output returns an operation ID. Use the xref:reference:rpk/rpk-cluster/rpk-cluster-config-status.adoc[`status`] command to check the progress of the configuration change.
endif::[]

ifndef::env-cloud[]
If you set the cluster property value to an empty string, the property is reset to its default.

This command is provided for use in scripts. For interactive editing, or bulk
changes, use the `edit` and `import` commands respectively.
endif::[]

== Usage

[,bash]
----
rpk cluster config set <key> <value> [flags]
----




== Examples

This section provides examples of how to use `rpk cluster config set`.

=== Set a single property

Enable auditing

[,bash]
----
rpk cluster config set audit_enabled true
----

=== Set multiple properties

Enable Iceberg with REST catalog type (use `key=value` notation)

[,bash]
----
rpk cluster config set iceberg_enabled=true iceberg_catalog_type=rest
----

== Flags

[cols="1m,1a,2a"]
|===
|Value |Type |Description

|--no-confirm |bool |Disable confirmation prompt.
|--timeout |duration |Maximum time to poll for operation completion before displaying operation ID for manual status checking (for example, 300ms, 1.5s, 30s).
|===

== Global flags

[cols="1m,1a,2a"]
|===
|Value |Type |Description

|--config |string |Redpanda or `rpk` config file; default search paths are `/Users/jakecahill/Library/Application Support/rpk/rpk.yaml`, `$PWD/redpanda.yaml`, and `/etc/redpanda/redpanda.yaml`.
|-X, --config-opt |stringArray |Override `rpk` configuration settings; `-X help` for detail or `-X list` for terser detail.
|--ignore-profile |bool |Ignore `rpk.yaml` and `redpanda.yaml`; use default settings.
|--profile |string |`rpk` profile to use.
|-v, --verbose |bool |Enable verbose logging.
|===

[NOTE]
====
Setting properties to non-number values (such as setting string values with `-`) can be problematic for some terminals due to how POSIX flags are parsed. For example, the following command may not work from some terminals:

[,bash]
----
rpk cluster config set delete_retention_ms -1
----

Workaround: Use `--` to disable parsing for all subsequent characters. For example:

[,bash]
----
rpk cluster config set -- delete_retention_ms -1
----
====


== Suggested reading

* xref:reference:properties/cluster-properties.adoc[Cluster Properties]
